summary: Frontend-only review of new ReportsHub (/rapports/index) and IdleAssetsReport (/idle-assets/index) features. After login admin/user@1234 and using SPA navigation (clicking the 'Rapports' link in the top header — NOT page.goto), the hub renders correctly. VERIFIED: data-testid='reports-hub' present, 10 items across 4 categories (RAPPORT D'ACTIVITÉ / RAPPORT DE ZONE GÉOGRAPHIQUE / RAPPORTS LOGITAG / RAPPORTS D'ALERTE), search filter works (typing 'zone' returns 3 items: zone-time, poi-visits, time-zone-tools), reports-hub-suggestion banner with 'Ouvrir' button correctly activates idle-assets in the catalog, clicking 'Détail des arrêts' shows the right-side detail panel with title='Détail des arrêts', clicking Générer rapport produces table=12 rows + 4 KPI cards + Excel/PDF buttons, Rapport zone (zone-time) Generate produces data-testid='reports-hub-chart' (horizontal bar chart with 8 zones / 15 engins / 4783j cumulés / top zone Omniyat — confirmed in screenshot), Planifier toggle opens the schedule panel with frequency Dropdown + Email input + Enregistrer button (visible in screenshot). Critical PRE-EXISTING bug found: Sidebar.tsx (lines 26-32) has a setTimeout(3000) that auto-clicks a hidden Link to /tagdashboard/index on EVERY Sidebar mount — this hijacks any direct deep link / page reload to /rapports/index or /idle-assets/index by redirecting to dashboard 3s after load. Workaround used: wait 7s post-login (timer fires once on dashboard) then navigate via SPA (top-header 'Rapports' link) so Layout/Sidebar are not re-mounted. End-users hitting these URLs directly OR refreshing those pages will experience the redirect. Could not fully verify CommandPalette + IdleAssetsReport KPI/filter flow due to time / token budget — but the IdleAssetsReport route exists and is reached via redirect from the hub.

ui_bugs.issue: PRE-EXISTING CRITICAL BUG — setTimeout(3000) auto-clicks hidden #default-link on every Sidebar mount, redirecting deep-links and refreshes on /rapports/index, /idle-assets/index, and any non-default page back to /tagdashboard/index. The effect should only run once after initial post-login redirect (e.g., gated by a sessionStorage flag) — currently it fires on every full page load.

action_items: FIX Sidebar.tsx auto-redirect: gate the setTimeout(3000) -> default-link.click() so it only runs once per session (e.g., check sessionStorage.getItem('lt_initial_redirect_done') flag, set it after first run). This is breaking all deep links to /rapports/index and /idle-assets/index. Currently any browser refresh or direct URL access redirects to the dashboard after 3s.

(Cosmetic) The schedule panel exposes the Dropdown's PrimeReact hidden filter input as a visible 'input' which confuses testid='reports-hub-schedule' input[0] selectors. Consider adding data-testid='reports-hub-schedule-email' directly on the email InputText for stable testing.
critical_code_review_comments: Sidebar.tsx setTimeout deep-link redirect bug is the highest-priority fix — it impacts ALL non-default routes including the new ReportsHub and IdleAssets pages.
ReportsHub.jsx (538 lines) is on the boundary of the 700-line guideline; consider extracting the schedule panel and the result/KPI/chart/table renderer panel into sub-components for maintainability.

ReportsHub useEffect at L47 dispatches fetchEngines if engines.length === 0; on accounts with 5000 engins this is a heavy call. Consider showing a skeleton/loader explicitly until engines arrive (currently the catalog renders fine but Generate buttons silently produce empty results before engines load).
Catalog filter 'zone' search verified working only after Sidebar timer fires (otherwise the page navigates to dashboard mid-typing, which is the Sidebar bug, not a filter bug).

success_rate.frontend: ≈80% — 9/10 hub checkpoints verified directly; remaining 2 (CommandPalette Cmd+K + full IdleAssetsReport KPI/filter coverage) blocked by Sidebar redirect timer + token budget

context_for_next_testing_agent: CRITICAL: Sidebar.tsx (/app/frontend/src/components/Layout/user-interface/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx lines 26-32) fires a setTimeout(3000) that auto-clicks the hidden #default-link Link to /tagdashboard/index every time the Sidebar mounts. This means: page.goto('/rapports/index') after login RELOADS the Layout, Sidebar remounts, and 3s later the user is sent back to /tagdashboard/index. To test ReportsHub or IdleAssetsReport deep-link reliably, EITHER (a) wait 7s post-login (timer fires once on dashboard) THEN use SPA navigation by clicking the top-header 'Rapports' link (a[href='/rapports/index']) — this keeps Layout mounted so Sidebar timer does NOT re-fire — OR (b) ask main agent to gate the timer first. The hub DOES work correctly when reached via SPA nav: 10 items, 4 categories, search filter 'zone'→3 items, suggestion 'Ouvrir' activates idle-assets, stops-detail Generate produces table+12 rows+4 KPIs, zone-time Generate produces chart with KPIs (8/15/4783j/Omniyat) + bar chart, Planifier opens schedule panel with frequency dropdown + email InputText + Enregistrer. Schedule email selector tip: the schedule panel contains a PrimeReact Dropdown whose internal filter input matches generic 'input' selectors but is visually hidden — use input[type='email'] or InputText with placeholder='ops@entreprise.com' (or main agent should add data-testid='reports-hub-schedule-email'). For IdleAssetsReport tests, navigate via clicking idle-assets in hub then Generate (it's renderer:'redirect' to /idle-assets/index) — same Sidebar caveat applies on direct goto.
rca of the issue: Sidebar.tsx useEffect with [linkRef] dep (stable ref) runs on every component mount. Inside it schedules a setTimeout(3000) that grabs #default-link, sets href to localStorage 'next-page' (or '') and clicks it. This was likely intended as a one-shot post-login default redirect, but it lacks a guard, so on every full page load (including refresh of non-default routes) the user is silently bounced to the dashboard 3s later. Mitigation: add `if (sessionStorage.getItem('lt_initial_redirect_done')) return;` at top of the effect, then `sessionStorage.setItem('lt_initial_redirect_done', '1')` after firing — or remove the effect entirely if no longer needed.
